title: Options after losing bank card — Vietnam-trip cash-access scramble
html:
TL;DR

  • Late-August 2022 contingency note: lost his bank card in vietnam and is improvising — get a Vietnamese card and transfer from rogue credit union, use the discover it card, or have someone Venmo him + give cash.
  • Trailing line "350k 8h 30people" — likely a shorthand for a tour pricing rate (350k VND ≈ $14 USD, 8 hours, 30 people) — possibly a ha giang loop tour quote or other Vietnam group activity.
  • Datapoint on Yahir's solo-traveler payments-resilience mindset: lose card → enumerate three workarounds → execute. No panic-spiral.
  • Confirms rogue credit union as Yahir's primary US bank in 2022 (Grants Pass-area credit union).
  • Pairs with "about to get fucked over by the Vietnamese family that helped me" — same trip, same week, harder texture.

Key fragment

"350k 8h 30people"

Why it matters

Two small but useful nodes:

  1. Banking footprint: rogue credit union + discover it card in 2022 — useful for any future questions about Yahir's financial-infrastructure history.
  2. Solo-travel resilience: the three-option enumeration (Vietnamese card / Discover / Venmo-cash) is the same problem-decomposition pattern that shows up in 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream and the founder grind notes — enumerate, pick, move.

The "Vietnamese family" line in the companion note suggests the lost-card may have been related to that conflict.

Open questions

  • Did the Venmo workaround actually work in vietnam (cross-currency / WiFi)?
  • Was the lost card stolen, or genuinely misplaced?
  • Did the Vietnamese family help/hurt situation tie to the lost card directly? (Speculative — see 2022 09 04 i have never been in love.)
